Details: One bench-scale study showed effective oxidation of terbufos with chlorination at doses typically applied at drinking water plants. In chlorinated water, terbufos was not detected within one hour of contact time. The degradation of terbufos in non-chlorinated water was attributed to hydrolysis. Two analysis methods were employed in the study. Significantly lower concentrations with the LC-MS/MS method were attributed to the instability of terbufos in water, since these samples remained in water before injection.
